methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series A → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.0x) to $234M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same-stage peers in Chips & Semiconductors. Range: $500M - $5.0B.

methods.name: Public-Comp Anchoring
methods.skip_reason: target revenue not on file (private companies rarely disclose)
methods.rationale: Public Semiconductors P/S median is 24.4x across 3 tickers. Cannot apply without target's revenue.

private.name: Etched
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $5B

private.name: d-Matrix
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $2B

private.name: Substrate
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $1B+

private.name: Rain AI
private.rationale: Same sector (Chips & Semiconductors), same stage (Series A), post-money $500M

data.narrative:
Black Semiconductor, a Series A chip design company founded by Daniel and Sebastian Schall, is evaluated against a universe of 8 private peers and 3 public semiconductors comparables (ALAB, NVDA, AMD). The private peer set includes Etched, d-Matrix, Substrate, Rain AI, and Cerebras, providing direct stage and sector alignment.

Two valuation methods are applicable. Stage-progression, using the $234M total raised as a proxy for implied post-money value, applies a typical Series A step-up multiplier of 2.0x, yielding $468M. Peer-median triangulation across four same-stage, same-sector companies (Etched at $5.0B, d-Matrix at $2.0B, Substrate at $1.0B, Rain AI at $500M) produces a median of $1.5B, with a range of $500M to $5.0B. Public-comp anchoring via P/S multiples (sector median 24.4x across three tickers) cannot be applied due to unavailable revenue data. Blending the two applicable methods with equal weighting yields a triangulated range of $398M (low) to $1.7B (high), with a median of $984M. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion across private peers—a 10x spread—and the absence of the target's revenue, which would enable public-market cross-validation and reduce valuation bandwidth materially.
